title: [CRITICAL] Harden save_session_summary against path traversal
description: Deep sweep finding: packages/moe-daemon/src/knowledge/MemoryManager.ts writes session summaries to `${workerId}_${taskId}.json` without validating either value, and saveSessionSummaryTool does not require worker/task existence. A malicious MCP caller can use path separators in workerId/taskId to write outside `.moe/memory/sessions`. Also getLastSession matches filenames by substring, so task IDs like task-1 can read task-10 summaries.
definitionOfDone: save_session_summary validates workerId/taskId with safe ID rules and rejects traversal/invalid IDs.
Session summary writes are guaranteed to stay under `.moe/memory/sessions` using resolved path checks or sanitized filenames.
getLastSession only matches exact task IDs rather than substring collisions.
Regression tests cover invalid IDs/path traversal and task-1 vs task-10 lookup.
packages/moe-daemon npm test and build pass.

implementationPlan.description: Add explicit workerId/taskId validation and path containment checks in save_session_summary/MemoryManager session file helpers; return clear MoeError invalid-input error handling for bad IDs instead of writing files.
implementationPlan.note: Added safe entity ID validation for session summary workerId/taskId, required existing worker/task in moe.save_session_summary, and resolved-path containment checks before writing under .moe/memory/sessions. Targeted tools.test passed (146 tests).

implementationPlan.description: Change getLastSession lookup to parse session summary files and match exact taskId rather than filename substring; keep malformed-file error handling fail-safe.
implementationPlan.note: Changed getLastSession to scan JSON files, parse each fail-safe, and return only summaries whose parsed session.taskId exactly matches the requested taskId; malformed files and invalid dates are skipped. Targeted tools.test passed (146 tests).

implementationPlan.description: Add regression tests for path traversal IDs, nonexistent/invalid IDs, and task-1 vs task-10 exact matching; run daemon test/build validation.
implementationPlan.note: Added regressions for valid saves staying under memory/sessions, traversal-shaped worker/task IDs rejected without outside writes, syntactically valid nonexistent worker/task IDs rejected, MemoryManager direct traversal rejected, and exact task-1 vs task-10 getLastSession matching with malformed JSON skipped. Validation: npx vitest run src/tools/tools.test.ts (150 passed), npm test (24 files/406 tests passed), npm run build (tsc passed). Adversarial review considered path traversal, invalid input, nonexistent entities, malformed files, and write failure paths.

comments.author: architect-2992
comments.content: Token-budget check: while fixing session summaries, keep saved summaries bounded/explicit; do not auto-save generic noise, and make invalid-ID errors compact (no echoed paths/payload dumps).

planningNotes.approachesConsidered: Preferred: validate workerId/taskId with validateEntityId and use an exact manifest/filename convention. Rejected: silently sanitizing IDs, because it can make summaries unreadable and hide malicious input.
planningNotes.codebaseInsights: Entity JSON writes go through StateManager.writeEntity, which already calls validateEntityId and resolved-path checks. MemoryManager session summaries bypass that helper and therefore need equivalent ID/path validation.
planningNotes.risks: Do not break existing session summary lookup for valid generated worker/task IDs. Include error handling for invalid IDs and filesystem write failures; tests should not depend on the wrapper's MOE_WORKER_ID env leak.
